title: senecio atratus
intro: Senecio atratus, also known as the "Night-blooming Groundsel," is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its unique ability to bloom in the evening and close its flowers by morning. The leaves and flowers of the Senecio atratus plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Senecio atratus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness their medicinal properties and take advantage of their health benefits.
